client_like.h(3) Library Functions Manual client_like.h(3)

NAME

client_like.h - Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct session_client_like_config

Typedefs


typedef struct tcp_client tcp_client_t
typedef struct websocket_client websocket_client_t
typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)
typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Functions


tcp_client_t * session_client_like_get_tcp_client (void)
websocket_client_t * session_client_like_get_websocket_client (void)
void session_client_like_set_websocket_client (websocket_client_t *client)
terminal_fd_reader_t * session_client_like_get_stdin_reader (void)
asciichat_error_t session_client_like_run (const session_client_like_config_t *config)

Variables


bool(*)(void *) session_client_like_get_render_should_exit (void)

Detailed Description

Shared initialization and teardown for client-like modes (mirror, client, discovery).

Provides a unified interface for running client-like modes that share common initialization patterns: media source selection, FPS probing, audio initialization, display creation, splash screen management, keepawake handling, and cleanup.

Each mode registers mode-specific callbacks to handle initialization, the main render loop, and keyboard input. The shared layer handles all the boilerplate.

Modes Supported

  • Mirror mode: Local webcam/media playback without networking
  • Client mode: Network client with per-connection initialization
  • Discovery mode: P2P mode with role negotiation

Shared Responsibilities

This layer automatically handles:

  • Terminal output management (stdout piping detection)
  • Keepawake system (platform sleep prevention)
  • Splash screen lifecycle and animation
  • Media source selection and FPS probing
  • Audio initialization and lifecycle
  • Display context creation and management
  • Render loop execution
  • Proper cleanup ordering (critical for PortAudio)

Mode-Specific Responsibilities

Mode files provide:

  • Main loop callback (run_fn) that calls session_render_loop() or custom loop
  • Optional network interrupt callback for socket shutdown
  • Optional custom exit condition for additional shutdown criteria
  • Keyboard handler for interactive controls
  • Terminal behavior preferences (newline on exit)

Memory and Lifecycle

All allocations (capture, display, audio) are owned and cleaned up by session_client_like_run(). Mode callbacks receive initialized, ready-to-use contexts and should not attempt to free them.

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

February 2026

Definition in file client_like.h.

Typedef Documentation

typedef struct session_client_like_config session_client_like_config_t

Configuration for session_client_like_run()

All media, display, audio, FPS, and snapshot settings are read automatically from GET_OPTION() inside the function. Callers only provide the fields below that differ per mode.

typedef asciichat_error_t(* session_client_like_run_fn) (session_capture_ctx_t *capture, session_display_ctx_t *display, void *user_data)

Mode-specific main loop callback

Called after all shared initialization is complete (capture opened, audio started, display ready, splash done). The mode runs its main loop here and returns when finished. The shared teardown runs after this returns.

Typical pattern:

// For mirror/discovery participant:
return session_render_loop(capture, display, exit_check_fn, NULL, NULL,


                           keyboard_handler, user_data);
// For client mode (protocol-driven):
while (!should_exit()) {


  // protocol loop...
}
return ASCIICHAT_OK;

Parameters

capture Initialized capture context. Never NULL.
display Initialized display context. Never NULL.
user_data User data passed through from config.run_user_data

Returns

ASCIICHAT_OK, or error code to abort (cleanup still runs)

Definition at line 95 of file client_like.h.

typedef struct tcp_client tcp_client_t

Definition at line 60 of file client_like.h.

typedef struct websocket_client websocket_client_t

Definition at line 61 of file client_like.h.

Function Documentation

tcp_client_t * session_client_like_get_tcp_client (void )

Get the TCP client created by session_client_like_run() (if applicable).

Returns the TCP client created for direct TCP connections (non-WebSocket). Only valid after session_client_like_run() is called and during run_fn execution. May be NULL if WebSocket is being used instead.

Returns

Pointer to tcp_client_t, or NULL if not created or using WebSocket

Definition at line 57 of file client_like.c.

57                                                        {
58   return g_tcp_client;
59 }








websocket_client_t *
  session_client_like_get_websocket_client (void )


Get the WebSocket client created by
    session_client_like_run() (if applicable).




Returns the WebSocket client created for WebSocket connections.
    Only valid after session_client_like_run() is called and during
    run_fn execution. May be NULL if TCP is being used instead.




Returns


Pointer to websocket_client_t, or NULL if not
  created or using TCP


Definition at line 61 of file client_like.c.


61                                                                    {
62   return g_websocket_client;
63 }










asciichat_error_t
  session_client_like_run (const session_client_like_config_t *
  config)


Run a client-like mode with fully shared initialization and
    teardown




This function orchestrates the complete lifecycle of client-like
    modes:








Initialization
  (automatic from GET_OPTION())


Terminal:


    
  
  • Forces stderr when stdout is piped (terminal_should_force_stderr)
    • 
  
  • Disables terminal logging during rendering, re-enables on exit
    • 
  
  • Handles snapshot mode log visibility
    • 



Keepawake:


    
  
  • Validates --keepawake / --no-keepawake mutual exclusivity
    • 
  
  • Calls platform_enable_keepawake() unless --no-keepawake set
    • 
  
  • Disables keepawake on exit
    • 



Splash Screen:


    
  
  • Creates temporary display context
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_start() to show splash animation
    • 
  
  • Holds splash visible during media probing and audio init
    • 
  
  • Calls splash_intro_done() when capture and display are ready
    • 



Media / Webcam
    (session_capture_ctx_t):



  
•

  
Selects source type from options (priority order):





  
 1.

  
--url → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE (FFmpeg handles http/rtsp/rtmp)

  
 2.

  
--file → MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE; "-" →
      MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN

  
 3.

  
--test-pattern → MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST

  
 4.

  
(default) → MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM




    
  
  • FPS: uses GET_OPTION(fps) if > 0, otherwise probes with temporary media
      source
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp) as initial seek
    • 
  
  • Applies GET_OPTION(media_loop) for file looping
    • 
  
  • Calls session_capture_create() to open webcam or media file
    • 
  
  • Destroys capture on exit
    • 



Audio (audio_context_t):


    
  
  • Skipped entirely for immediate snapshots (snapshot_mode &&
      snapshot_delay == 0)
    • 
  
  • Probes for audio via media_source_has_audio() on capture's
    source
    • 
  
  • Allocates and initializes audio_context_t when audio available
    • 
  
  • Links audio context to capture and media source
    • 
  
  • Sets playback_only based on audio_should_enable_microphone()
    • 
  
  • Disables jitter buffering for local file playback
    • 
  
  • Calls audio_start_duplex() after splash_intro_done()
    • 
  
  • Stops, destroys, and frees audio on exit
    • 



Display (session_display_ctx_t):


    
  
  • Calls session_display_create() with snapshot and color
    settings
    • 
  
  • Passes audio context if initialized
    • 
  
  • Destroys display on exit
    • 







Mode-Specific
  Loop


Calls config->run_fn(capture, display,
    config->run_user_data) once all initialization is complete. This is where
    each mode calls session_render_loop() or runs its own
    protocol-thread-driven loop.








Cleanup
  (always runs, even on error)


Cleanup sequence (order is critical):



  
1.

  
Re-enable terminal logging

  
2.

  
audio_terminate_portaudio_final() - Free PortAudio device resources
      FIRST

  
3.

  
audio_stop_duplex() + audio_destroy() + SAFE_FREE

  
4.

  
session_display_destroy()

  
5.

  
session_capture_destroy()

  
6.

  
webcam_destroy() - Free cached webcam images and test patterns

  
7.

  
session_log_buffer_destroy()

  
8.

  
platform_disable_keepawake()

  
9.

  
Print '
    

  




Parameters


config Mode configuration. Must not be NULL.
  run_fn must not be NULL.


Returns


ASCIICHAT_OK on success, or the first error code from
  initialization or from run_fn. Cleanup always runs regardless of return
  value.


Definition at line 107 of file client_like.c.


107                                                                                       {
108   if (!config || !config->run_fn) {
109     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "config or run_fn is NULL");
110   }
111 
112   // Store config globally for adapter callbacks
113   g_current_config = config;
114 
115   // Store the render should_exit adapter globally so mode-specific run_fn can access it
116   g_render_should_exit = display_should_exit_adapter;
117 
118   asciichat_error_t result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
119 
120   // Keep track of what's been initialized for cleanup
121   session_capture_ctx_t *capture = NULL;
122   session_display_ctx_t *display = NULL;
123   audio_context_t *audio_ctx = NULL;
124   media_source_t *probe_source = NULL;
125   bool audio_available = false;
126 
127   // ============================================================================
128   // SETUP: Terminal and Logging
129   // ============================================================================
130 
131   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up terminal and logging");
132 
133   // Force stderr when stdout is piped (prevents ASCII corruption in output stream)
134   bool should_force_stderr = terminal_should_force_stderr();
135   log_debug("terminal_should_force_stderr()=%d", should_force_stderr);
136 
137   if (should_force_stderr) {
138     // Redirect logs to stderr to prevent corruption of stdout (for pipes)
139     // But keep terminal output enabled so we can see initialization errors
140     log_set_force_stderr(true);
141   }
142 
143   // Note: Force all logging to stderr if encoding to pipe to prevent corrupting binary video output
144   // (implemented via should_force_stderr check above)
145   const char *render_file_early = GET_OPTION(render_file);
146   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_RUN] render_file_early=%s (ptr=%p)",
147            render_file_early ? render_file_early : "(null)", (void *)render_file_early);
148   if (render_file_early && render_file_early[0] != '\0' &&
149       (strcmp(render_file_early, "-") == 0 || strcmp(render_file_early, "pipe:") == 0)) {
150     log_info("--render-file=- will output binary video to stdout, logging sent to stderr");
151   }
152 
153   // ============================================================================
154   // SETUP: Keepawake System
155   // ============================================================================
156 
157   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Validating keepawake options");
158 
159   // Validate mutual exclusivity
160   bool enable_ka = GET_OPTION(enable_keepawake);
161   bool disable_ka = GET_OPTION(disable_keepawake);
162   log_debug("enable_keepawake=%d, disable_keepawake=%d", enable_ka, disable_ka);
163 
164   if (enable_ka && disable_ka) {
165     result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "--keepawake and --no-keepawake are mutually exclusive");
166     goto cleanup;
167   }
168 
169   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Enabling keepawake if needed");
170 
171   // Enable keepawake unless explicitly disabled
172   if (!disable_ka) {
173     (void)platform_enable_keepawake();
174   }
175 
176   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Keepawake setup complete");
177 
178   // ============================================================================
179   // SETUP: Display Context (before splash for unified TTY initialization)
180   // ============================================================================
181 
182   // Create display context early to share TTY initialization between splash and rendering
183   // This prevents double TTY init which was corrupting the terminal state
184   session_display_config_t display_config = {0};
185   display_config.snapshot_mode = GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode);
186   display_config.palette_type = GET_OPTION(palette_type);
187   display_config.custom_palette = GET_OPTION(palette_custom_set) ? GET_OPTION(palette_custom) : NULL;
188   display_config.color_mode = TERM_COLOR_AUTO;
189   display_config.should_exit_callback = display_should_exit_adapter;
190   display_config.callback_data = NULL;
191 
192   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Creating display context (before splash)");
193   display = session_display_create(&display_config);
194   if (!display) {
195     log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] session_display_create() returned NULL");
196     log_fatal("Failed to initialize display context");
197     result = ERROR_DISPLAY;
198     goto cleanup;
199   }
200   log_debug("[SETUP_DISPLAY_EARLY] Display context created, will use for splash");
201 
202   // ============================================================================
203   // SETUP: Splash Screen (using unified display context)
204   // ============================================================================
205 
206   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Starting splash with display context");
207 
208   // Use the display context we just created for splash animation
209   splash_intro_start(display);
210   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): splash_intro_start() returned");
211 
212   // Detect if we're using media vs webcam (needed for splash timing)
213   const char *media_url = GET_OPTION(media_url);
214   const char *media_file = GET_OPTION(media_file);
215   bool has_media = (media_url && strlen(media_url) > 0) || (media_file && strlen(media_file) > 0);
216 
217   // Show splash briefly for webcam, but skip sleep for media (which takes time anyway)
218   if (!has_media && !GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
219     platform_sleep_ms(250);
220   }
221   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): After splash sleep");
222 
223   // Restore stderr now that splash animation and post-splash logging are done
224   // This allows logs to appear on screen again after this point
225   splash_restore_stderr();
226 
227   // ============================================================================
228   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
229   // ============================================================================
230 
231   // ============================================================================
232   // SETUP: Terminal Logging in Snapshot Mode
233   // ============================================================================
234 
235   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): About to disable terminal logging (snapshot=%d)", GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode));
236   if (!GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode)) {
237     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Calling log_set_terminal_output(false)...");
238     log_set_terminal_output(false);
239     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): RETURNED from log_set_terminal_output(false)");
240   }
241   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Terminal logging disabled, starting media source setup");
242 
243   // ============================================================================
244   // SETUP: Media Source Selection and FPS Probing
245   // ============================================================================
246 
247   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Initializing capture config");
248   session_capture_config_t capture_config = {0};
249   capture_config.resize_for_network = false;
250   capture_config.should_exit_callback = capture_should_exit_adapter;
251   capture_config.callback_data = NULL;
252 
253   // Determine FPS explicitly set by user
254   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting FPS option");
255   int user_fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
256   bool fps_explicitly_set = user_fps > 0;
257   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): FPS=%d (explicitly_set=%d)", user_fps, fps_explicitly_set);
258 
259   // Select media source based on options (priority order)
260   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_url option");
261   const char *media_url_val = GET_OPTION(media_url);
262   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_file option");
263   const char *media_file_val = GET_OPTION(media_file);
264   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): media_url=%s, media_file=%s", media_url_val ? media_url_val : "(null)",
265             media_file_val ? media_file_val : "(null)");
266 
267   if (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) {
268     // Network URL takes priority
269     log_info("Using network URL: %s (webcam disabled)", media_url_val);
270     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
271     capture_config.path = media_url_val;
272     capture_config.loop = false; // Network URLs cannot be looped
273 
274     if (fps_explicitly_set) {
275       capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
276       log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
277     } else {
278       // Always probe FPS for HTTP URLs, even in snapshot mode
279       // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
280       log_debug("Probing FPS for HTTP URL");
281       probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_url_val);
282       if (probe_source) {
283         double url_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
284         log_info("Detected HTTP stream video FPS: %.1f", url_fps);
285         if (url_fps > 0.0) {
286           capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(url_fps + 0.5);
287         } else {
288           log_warn("FPS detection failed for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
289           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
290         }
291       } else {
292         log_warn("Failed to create probe source for HTTP stream, using default 60 FPS");
293         capture_config.target_fps = 60;
294       }
295     }
296   } else if (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) {
297     // Local file or stdin
298     log_info("DEBUG: media_file_val='%s', strcmp result=%d", media_file_val, strcmp(media_file_val, "-"));
299     if (strcmp(media_file_val, "-") == 0) {
300       log_info("Using stdin for media streaming (webcam disabled)");
301       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_STDIN;
302       capture_config.path = NULL;
303       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
304       capture_config.loop = false;
305     } else {
306       log_info("Using media file: %s (webcam disabled)", media_file_val);
307       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE;
308       capture_config.path = media_file_val;
309       capture_config.loop = GET_OPTION(media_loop);
310 
311       if (fps_explicitly_set) {
312         capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)user_fps;
313         log_info("Using user-specified FPS: %u", capture_config.target_fps);
314       } else {
315         // Always probe FPS for local files, even in snapshot mode
316         // (snapshot mode needs correct FPS to capture right number of frames during snapshot_delay window)
317         log_debug("Probing FPS for local file");
318         probe_source = media_source_create(MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE, media_file_val);
319         if (probe_source) {
320           double file_fps = media_source_get_video_fps(probe_source);
321           log_info("Detected file video FPS: %.1f", file_fps);
322           if (file_fps > 0.0) {
323             capture_config.target_fps = (uint32_t)(file_fps + 0.5);
324           } else {
325             log_warn("FPS detection failed, using default 60 FPS");
326             capture_config.target_fps = 60;
327           }
328         } else {
329           log_warn("Failed to create probe source for FPS detection, using default 60 FPS");
330           capture_config.target_fps = 60;
331         }
332       }
333     }
334   } else if (GET_OPTION(test_pattern)) {
335     // Test pattern
336     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Using test pattern");
337     log_info("Using test pattern");
338     capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
339     capture_config.path = NULL;
340     capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
341     capture_config.loop = false;
342   } else {
343     // Default: Try webcam if stdout is a TTY, otherwise use test pattern
344     // (webcam + piped output often fails, test pattern is reliable fallback)
345     bool stdout_is_tty = terminal_is_stdout_tty();
346     if (stdout_is_tty) {
347       // Normal terminal mode: use webcam
348       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is TTY, using local webcam");
349       log_info("Using local webcam");
350       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_WEBCAM;
351       capture_config.path = NULL;
352       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
353       capture_config.loop = false;
354     } else {
355       // Piped output mode: use test pattern (webcam + pipes often fails)
356       log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Stdout is piped, defaulting to test pattern for reliability");
357       log_info("Stdout is piped - using test pattern instead of webcam");
358       capture_config.type = MEDIA_SOURCE_TEST;
359       capture_config.path = NULL;
360       capture_config.target_fps = fps_explicitly_set ? (uint32_t)user_fps : 60;
361       capture_config.loop = false;
362     }
363   }
364 
365   // Apply initial seek if specified
366   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Getting media_seek_timestamp option");
367   capture_config.initial_seek_timestamp = GET_OPTION(media_seek_timestamp);
368   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Media seek timestamp set");
369 
370   // Clean up probe source (don't reuse for actual capture)
371   if (probe_source) {
372     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Destroying probe source");
373     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
374     probe_source = NULL;
375     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Probe source destroyed");
376   } else {
377     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): No probe source to destroy");
378   }
379 
380   // Pass probed FPS to display context for correct render-file duration
381   if (capture_config.target_fps > 0) {
382     session_display_set_render_fps(display, capture_config.target_fps);
383     log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Set display render FPS to %u for correct video encoding duration",
384               capture_config.target_fps);
385   }
386 
387   // ============================================================================
388   // SETUP: Network Transports (TCP/WebSocket)
389   // ============================================================================
390 
391   log_debug("session_client_like_run(): Setting up network transports");
392 
393   // Use network clients from config if provided (client mode), or skip for mirror mode
394   // Mirror mode passes NULL for both tcp_client and websocket_client
395   g_tcp_client = config->tcp_client;
396   g_websocket_client = config->websocket_client;
397 
398   // Determine the networking mode based on available indicators:
399   // - If config has a discovery object, it's discovery mode (will create network clients later)
400   // - If config already has tcp_client or websocket_client, it's network mode (client mode)
401   // - Otherwise, it's mirror mode (local-only capture)
402   // Mirror mode has a run_fn (mirror_run) but no network components
403   bool discovery_mode = (config->discovery != NULL);
404   bool network_mode = (g_tcp_client != NULL || g_websocket_client != NULL);
405   bool mirror_mode = (!discovery_mode && !network_mode);
406 
407   if (mirror_mode) {
408     log_debug("Mirror mode detected - will use local capture with media source");
409   } else if (discovery_mode) {
410     log_debug("Discovery mode detected - discovery session will manage networking");
411   } else if (network_mode) {
412     log_debug("Client/Network mode detected - will use network capture without local media source");
413   }
414 
415   // ============================================================================
416   // SETUP: Capture Context
417   // ============================================================================
418 
419   // Check for stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, render to video
420   // Only enable stdin render mode if:
421   // 1. render-file is "-" (output to stdout)
422   // 2. stdin is not a TTY
423   // 3. --height is explicitly specified (user must opt-in to stdin render mode)
424   // 4. NO media source is being used (not reading from file/URL/test-pattern)
425   // This prevents accidental activation when piping to other processes or using mirror mode
426   const char *render_file_opt = GET_OPTION(render_file);
427   int frame_height = GET_OPTION(height);
428   bool height_explicitly_set = (frame_height != OPT_HEIGHT_DEFAULT);
429   bool has_media_source = (media_url_val && strlen(media_url_val) > 0) ||
430                           (media_file_val && strlen(media_file_val) > 0) ||
431                           GET_OPTION(test_pattern);
432   bool stdin_render_mode = (render_file_opt && strcmp(render_file_opt, "-") == 0 &&
433                             !terminal_is_stdin_tty() && height_explicitly_set &&
434                             !has_media_source && !mirror_mode);
435 
436   if (stdin_render_mode) {
437     // Stdin render mode: read ASCII frames from stdin, output video to stdout
438     log_info("Stdin render mode enabled: reading ASCII frames from stdin, output to stdout");
439 
440     // Get frame dimensions (--height must be explicitly set to reach here)
441     int frame_width = GET_OPTION(width);
442 
443     // Validate stdin render mode constraints (--height is already validated above since height_explicitly_set is true)
444     bool width_was_explicitly_set = (frame_width != OPT_WIDTH_DEFAULT);
445 
446     if (width_was_explicitly_set) {
447       result = SET_ERRNO(ERROR_USAGE, "Stdin render mode does not accept --width (auto-detected from frames).\n"
448                                       "Only specify: --height <rows>");
449       goto cleanup;
450     }
451 
452     asciichat_error_t stdin_err = terminal_fd_reader_create(STDIN_FILENO, frame_height, &g_stdin_reader);
453     if (stdin_err != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
454       log_fatal("Failed to initialize stdin frame reader: %s", asciichat_error_string(stdin_err));
455       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
456       goto cleanup;
457     }
458 
459     // For stdin render mode, create a minimal capture context for compatibility
460     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
461     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
462     if (!capture) {
463       log_fatal("Failed to initialize capture context for stdin rendering");
464       terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
465       g_stdin_reader = NULL;
466       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
467       goto cleanup;
468     }
469 
470     log_info("stdin render mode: reading %d-line frames from stdin, auto-detecting width", frame_height);
471   }
472 
473   // Choose capture type based on mode determined earlier:
474   // - Mirror mode: needs to capture local media (webcam, file, test pattern)
475   // - Network modes (client/discovery): receive frames from network, no local capture
476   bool is_network_mode = network_mode || discovery_mode;
477 
478   if (!stdin_render_mode && is_network_mode) {
479     // Network mode: create minimal capture context without media source
480     log_debug("Network mode detected - using network capture (no local media source)");
481     int fps = GET_OPTION(fps);
482     uint64_t net_cap_start = time_get_ns();
483     capture = session_network_capture_create((uint32_t)(fps > 0 ? fps : 60));
484     double net_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(net_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
485     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Network capture created in %.1f ms", net_cap_ms);
486     if (!capture) {
487       log_fatal("Failed to initialize network capture context");
488       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
489       goto cleanup;
490     }
491     if (fps > 0) {
492       log_debug("Network capture FPS set to %d from options", fps);
493     }
494   }
495 
496   // ============================================================================
497   // SETUP: Audio Context
498   // ============================================================================
499 
500   // Skip audio for immediate snapshots
501   bool should_init_audio = true;
502   if (GET_OPTION(snapshot_mode) && GET_OPTION(snapshot_delay) == 0.0) {
503     should_init_audio = false;
504     log_debug("Skipping audio initialization for immediate snapshot");
505   }
506 
507   // Probe for audio
508   if (should_init_audio && capture_config.type == MEDIA_SOURCE_FILE && capture_config.path) {
509     media_source_t *audio_probe_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
510     if (audio_probe_source && media_source_has_audio(audio_probe_source)) {
511       audio_available = true;
512 
513       // Allocate and initialize audio context
514       audio_ctx = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(audio_context_t), audio_context_t *);
515       if (audio_ctx) {
516         *audio_ctx = (audio_context_t){0};
517         if (audio_init(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
518           // Link audio to media source
519           media_source_t *media_source = session_capture_get_media_source(capture);
520           audio_ctx->media_source = media_source;
521 
522           if (media_source) {
523             media_source_set_audio_context(media_source, audio_ctx);
524           }
525 
526           // Determine if microphone should be enabled
527           bool should_enable_mic = audio_should_enable_microphone(GET_OPTION(audio_source), audio_available);
528           audio_ctx->playback_only = !should_enable_mic;
529 
530           // Disable jitter buffering for file playback
531           if (audio_ctx->playback_buffer) {
532             audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_enabled = false;
533             atomic_store_bool(&audio_ctx->playback_buffer->jitter_buffer_filled, true);
534           }
535 
536           // Store in capture for keyboard handler access
537           session_capture_set_audio_context(capture, audio_ctx);
538 
539           log_debug("Audio context initialized");
540         } else {
541           log_warn("Failed to initialize audio context");
542           audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
543           SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
544           audio_ctx = NULL;
545           audio_available = false;
546         }
547       }
548     }
549   }
550 
551   // Pass stdin_reader to display if in stdin render mode
552   if (stdin_render_mode && g_stdin_reader) {
553     session_display_set_stdin_reader(display, g_stdin_reader);
554     log_debug("stdin_reader passed to display context");
555   }
556 
557   // ============================================================================
558   // SETUP: End Splash Screen
559   // ============================================================================
560   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] About to end splash screen");
561 
562   // The splash screen will display during initialization and exit when done.
563   // The initialization process (media probing, display setup, etc.) naturally
564   // takes time, keeping the splash visible. The splash is kept alive during
565   // connection attempts (TCP and WebSocket) to prevent a blank screen during
566   // network operations. splash_intro_done() is called only after a successful
567   // connection is established.
568 
569   log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Splash will remain visible during connection attempts");
570 
571   // Exit early if shutdown was requested (e.g., user pressed Ctrl-C)
572   if (APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
573     log_debug("[SETUP] Shutdown requested, exiting early");
574     log_debug("[SETUP_SPLASH] Ending splash due to early shutdown request");
575     splash_intro_done();
576     splash_wait_for_animation();
577     result = ASCIICHAT_OK;
578     goto cleanup;
579   }
580 
581   // Wait for splash animation to finish before proceeding to render loop
582   // (splash_anim thread uses the display, must not be disrupted before cleanup)
583   splash_intro_done();
584   splash_wait_for_animation();
585 
586   // ============================================================================
587   // SETUP: Create Mirror Capture (after splash screen finishes)
588   // ============================================================================
589 
590   if (!stdin_render_mode && !capture) {
591     // Mirror mode: create capture context with local media source (NOW, after splash finishes)
592     log_debug("Mirror mode detected - creating mirror capture after splash screen");
593     uint64_t mirror_cap_start = time_get_ns();
594     capture = session_mirror_capture_create(&capture_config);
595     double mirror_cap_ms = time_ns_to_ms(time_elapsed_ns(mirror_cap_start, time_get_ns()));
596     log_info("★ INIT_CHECKPOINT: Mirror capture created in %.1f ms (after splash)", mirror_cap_ms);
597     if (!capture) {
598       log_fatal("Failed to initialize mirror capture source");
599       result = ERROR_MEDIA_INIT;
600       goto cleanup;
601     }
602   }
603 
604   // ============================================================================
605   // SETUP: Start Audio Playback
606   // ============================================================================
607 
608   log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] About to check audio context for duplex");
609   if (audio_ctx && audio_available) {
610     log_debug("[SETUP_AUDIO] Starting audio duplex");
611     if (audio_start_duplex(audio_ctx) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
612       log_info("Audio playback started");
613     } else {
614       log_warn("Failed to start audio duplex");
615       audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
616       SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
617       audio_ctx = NULL;
618       audio_available = false;
619     }
620   }
621 
622   log_info("[MARKER] Audio setup complete at line 614");
623   log_info("[SETUP_COMPLETE] All setup complete, about to start connection loop");
624 
625   // ============================================================================
626   // RUN: Mode-Specific Main Loop with Reconnection
627   // ============================================================================
628 
629   // Connection/attempt loop - wraps run_fn with reconnection logic
630   int attempt = 0;
631   int max_attempts = config->max_reconnect_attempts;
632 
633   log_info("[CLIENT_LIKE_LOOP] Starting connection loop, max_attempts=%d", max_attempts);
634 
635   while (true) {
636     attempt++;
637 
638     result = config->run_fn(capture, display, config->run_user_data);
639 
640     // Exit immediately if run_fn succeeded
641     if (result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
642       // Connection succeeded - splash screen cleanup now happens when first frame renders
643       // This ensures the splash stays visible during TCP/WebSocket/datachannel connection attempts
644 
645       break;
646     }
647 
648     // Check if we should attempt reconnection
649     bool should_retry = false;
650 
651     if (max_attempts != 0) { // max_attempts == 0 means no retries
652       // Check custom reconnect logic if provided
653       if (config->should_reconnect_callback) {
654         should_retry = config->should_reconnect_callback(result, attempt, config->reconnect_user_data);
655       } else {
656         should_retry = true; // Default: always retry
657       }
658 
659       // Check retry limits if still planning to retry
660       if (should_retry && max_attempts > 0 && attempt >= max_attempts) {
661         log_debug("Reached maximum reconnection attempts (%d), giving up", max_attempts);
662         should_retry = false;
663       }
664     }
665 
666     // If not retrying or shutdown requested, exit loop
667     if (!should_retry || APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
668       break;
669     }
670 
671     // Reconnection will happen - show splash screen during reconnection attempt
672     // Reset first_frame flag so splash cleanup runs on next successful connection
673     session_display_reset_first_frame(display);
674 
675     // splash_intro_start() handles all the logic:
676     // - If splash should display: shows splash and disables console logging
677     // - If splash shouldn't display: restores stderr and returns without changing logging state
678     splash_intro_start(display);
679 
680     // Log reconnection message
681     if (max_attempts == -1) {
682       log_info("Connection failed, retrying...");
683     } else if (max_attempts > 0) {
684       log_info("Connection failed, retrying (attempt %d/%d)...", attempt + 1, max_attempts);
685     }
686 
687     // Apply reconnection delay if configured
688     // Check APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit) frequently during sleep so SIGTERM can interrupt reconnection attempts
689     if (config->reconnect_delay_ms > 0) {
690       unsigned int remaining_ms = config->reconnect_delay_ms;
691       const unsigned int check_interval_ms = 100; // Check exit flag every 100ms
692 
693       while (remaining_ms > 0 && !APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
694         unsigned int sleep_ms = (remaining_ms < check_interval_ms) ? remaining_ms : check_interval_ms;
695         platform_sleep_ms(sleep_ms);
696         remaining_ms -= sleep_ms;
697       }
698     }
699 
700     // Continue loop to retry
701   }
702 
703   // ============================================================================
704   // CLEANUP (always runs, even on error)
705   // ============================================================================
706 
707 cleanup:
708   // Re-enable terminal output for shutdown logs
709   log_set_terminal_output(true);
710 
711   // Cleanup network transports (TCP/WebSocket clients)
712   if (g_websocket_client) {
713     log_debug("Destroying WebSocket client");
714     websocket_client_destroy(&g_websocket_client);
715   }
716   if (g_tcp_client) {
717     log_debug("Destroying TCP client");
718     tcp_client_destroy(&g_tcp_client);
719   }
720 
721   // CRITICAL: Terminate PortAudio device resources FIRST
722   log_debug("Terminating PortAudio device resources");
723   audio_terminate_portaudio_final();
724 
725   // Stop audio thread before destroying audio context to prevent use-after-free
726   // The audio worker thread may still be logging when we destroy the buffer
727   APP_CALLBACK_VOID(audio_stop_thread);
728 
729   // Stop and destroy audio (after PortAudio is terminated and audio thread is stopped)
730   if (audio_ctx) {
731     audio_stop_duplex(audio_ctx);
732     audio_destroy(audio_ctx);
733     SAFE_FREE(audio_ctx);
734     audio_ctx = NULL;
735   }
736 
737   // Destroy display
738   if (display) {
739     session_display_destroy(display);
740     display = NULL;
741   }
742 
743   // Destroy capture
744   if (capture) {
745     session_capture_destroy(capture);
746     capture = NULL;
747   }
748 
749   // Destroy stdin reader if active (stdin render mode)
750   if (g_stdin_reader) {
751     terminal_fd_reader_destroy(g_stdin_reader);
752     g_stdin_reader = NULL;
753   }
754 
755   // Clean up probe source if still allocated (shouldn't happen, but be safe)
756   if (probe_source) {
757     media_source_destroy(probe_source);
758     probe_source = NULL;
759   }
760 
761   // Stop splash animation and enforce minimum display time (even on error path)
762   // But skip completely if shutting down - don't interact with splash at all during shutdown
763   // The animation thread will exit on its own when it detects shutdown_is_requested()
764   if (!APP_CALLBACK_BOOL(should_exit)) {
765     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_intro_done()");
766     splash_intro_done();
767     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_intro_done() returned");
768 
769     // Wait for animation thread to exit before cleanup
770     log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call splash_wait_for_animation()");
771     splash_wait_for_animation();
772     log_debug("[CLEANUP] splash_wait_for_animation() returned");
773   } else {
774     log_debug("[CLEANUP] Skipping all splash operations (shutdown in progress)");
775     // During shutdown, don't interact with splash - let animation thread exit naturally
776     // and don't wait for it (prevents blocking on signals)
777   }
778 
779   // Stop debug sync thread before destroying log buffer to prevent use-after-free
780   log_debug("[CLEANUP] About to call debug_sync_cleanup_thread()");
781   debug_sync_cleanup_thread();
782   log_debug("[CLEANUP] debug_sync_cleanup_thread() returned");
783 
784   // Disable keepawake (re-allow OS to sleep)
785   log_debug("[CLEANUP] Disabling keepawake");
786   platform_disable_keepawake();
787   log_debug("[CLEANUP] keepawake disabled");
788 
789   // Write newline to separate final frame from prompt (if configured)
790   log_debug("About to check print_newline_on_tty_exit");
791   if (config->print_newline_on_tty_exit && terminal_is_stdout_tty()) {
792     log_debug("Writing newline");
793     const char newline = '\n';
794     (void)platform_write_all(STDOUT_FILENO, &newline, 1);
795   }
796 
797   log_set_terminal_output(false);
798 
799   return result;
800 }






References atomic_store_bool(), audio_destroy(),
    audio_init(), audio_should_enable_microphone(),
    audio_start_duplex(), audio_stop_duplex(),
    audio_stop_thread(), audio_terminate_portaudio_final(),
    session_capture_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::callback_data,
    session_display_config_t::color_mode,
    session_display_config_t::custom_palette,
    debug_sync_cleanup_thread(),
    session_client_like_config::discovery,
    session_capture_config_t::initial_seek_timestamp,
    log_set_force_stderr(), log_set_terminal_output(),
    session_capture_config_t::loop,
    session_client_like_config::max_reconnect_attempts,
    media_source_create(), media_source_destroy(),
    media_source_get_video_fps(), media_source_has_audio(),
    media_source_set_audio_context(),
    session_display_config_t::palette_type,
    session_capture_config_t::path, platform_disable_keepawake(),
    platform_enable_keepawake(), platform_sleep_ms(),
    platform_write_all(),
    session_client_like_config::print_newline_on_tty_exit,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_delay_ms,
    session_client_like_config::reconnect_user_data,
    session_capture_config_t::resize_for_network,
    session_client_like_config::run_fn,
    session_client_like_config::run_user_data, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, session_capture_destroy(),
    session_capture_get_media_source(),
    session_capture_set_audio_context(), session_display_create(),
    session_display_destroy(),
    session_display_reset_first_frame(),
    session_display_set_render_fps(),
    session_display_set_stdin_reader(),
    session_mirror_capture_create(),
    session_network_capture_create(), SET_ERRNO,
    should_exit(), session_capture_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_display_config_t::should_exit_callback,
    session_client_like_config::should_reconnect_callback,
    session_display_config_t::snapshot_mode, splash_intro_done(),
    splash_intro_start(), splash_restore_stderr(),
    splash_wait_for_animation(),
    session_capture_config_t::target_fps,
    session_client_like_config::tcp_client, tcp_client_destroy(),
    terminal_fd_reader_create(), terminal_fd_reader_destroy(),
    terminal_is_stdin_tty(), terminal_is_stdout_tty(),
    terminal_should_force_stderr(), time_elapsed_ns(),
    time_get_ns(), session_capture_config_t::type,
    session_client_like_config::websocket_client, and
    websocket_client_destroy().


Referenced by client_main(), discovery_main(), and
    mirror_main().






void
  session_client_like_set_websocket_client (websocket_client_t *
  client)


Set the WebSocket client for this session.




Called by connection_attempt_tcp() after successfully
    creating a WebSocket client to update the global state. This ensures that
    session_client_like_run() correctly detects network mode instead of
    mirror mode.




Parameters


client WebSocket client instance, or NULL to
  clear


Definition at line 65 of file client_like.c.


65                                                                           {
66   g_websocket_client = client;
67 }












Variable
  Documentation




bool(*)(void *)
  session_client_like_get_render_should_exit(void) (void )


Get the render loop should_exit callback set by
    session_client_like_run().




This is used by mode-specific run_fn callbacks to obtain the
    proper should_exit callback for passing to session_render_loop(). The
    callback checks both the global should_exit() flag and any
    mode-specific custom_should_exit condition.




Returns


Function pointer to the render should_exit callback, or
  NULL if session_client_like_run() has not been called yet.


Definition at line 253 of file client_like.h.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
